intro: Eschweilera gracilipes, also known as the "Brazilian cherry tree," is a species of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for reducing pain and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Eschweilera gracilipes include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as terpenoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
